calendar.matters:
ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Mar-19-2019 Off Calendar

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-10-2018 Set For Trial On Jul-16-18 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

MASTER CALENDAR JURY ASBESTOS
Off Calendar Oct-22-2018 Set For Order To Show Cause On Apr-04-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 503

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Of Defendant Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc
Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-26-2018 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar May-22-2018 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Apr-10-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff and moving party Michael Starr and Teresa Starr
Laura Przetak of SPANOS | PRZETAK (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
Hanna M. Thompson of FOLEY & MANSFIELD (510-590-9500), appearing for defendant Parsons Government Services Inc. and Clark Reliance Corporation
Eric D. Sentlinger of PERKINS COIE (415-344-7000), appearing for defendant Genuine Parts Company
Andrea J. Casalett of DENTONS US LLP (415-267-4094), appearing for defendant and Joinder in Opposition Certainteed Corporation
Nell Moley of Alston & Birds LLP (415-243-1038), appearing for defendant and joinder in opposition Anheuser Busch
Emily M. Westfall of MANNING, GROSS & MASSENBURG (415) 527-2807, appearing for defendant and opposing party Temporary Plant Clearners, Inc.

RULING:
No argument and Court adopts the tentative ruling as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a) and (d). The trial date is July 16, 2018 at 11:15a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is August 8, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. Parties stipulate to Motions for summary judgment /summary adjudication may be heard on 55 days notice. Oppositions are due ten (10) days before the hearing. Replies are due five days before the hearing. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 12, 2018. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Plaintiffs have stipulated that Michael Starr will not be providing any testimony via deposition, declaration or at trial against defendants who answered on or before March 22, 2018. Plaintiff Teresa Starr must be produced for deposition to commence no later than April 25, 2018. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is June 29, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is July 13, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 4, 2018. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only.)
Off Calendar Apr-10-2018 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than April 20, 2018 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than April 20, 2018 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on May 23, 2018 at 1:30p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than June 1, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than June 15, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by 2:00 p.m. July 9, 2018. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, July 12, 2018 at 9:30 a.m. in 603. The responses filed by Pacific Gas and Electric Co., Anheuser-Busch, LLC, Temporary Plant Cleaners, Inc., Lamons Gasket Co. and Underground Construction Company, Inc. were not considered for failure to lodge courtesy copies with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7(B).) Order signed in open court. Judge: Lynn O'Malley-Taylor, Matter not reported. (503/LOT)
